methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Mindgrove Technologies is a Series A stage semiconductor company founded by Shashwath TR and Sharan Srinivas J, having raised ₹85 Cr (~$10M) to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private peers: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M), alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation approach given the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target. The 4-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. This method directly reflects current market pricing for companies at an identical development stage within the Chips & Semiconductors sector. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (24.4x median across the 3 public tickers) cannot be applied without revenue visibility, a common constraint in private company analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), anchored at $1.5B median. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single executable method. Key uncertainties include the dispersion within the peer set itself—a 10x range from Rain AI to Etched—and the absence of revenue or unit economics for Mindgrove, limiting assessment of relative competitive positioning. Stage, sector composition, and funding momentum appear to anchor pricing, but downstream operational traction remains unvalidated.
